Approach 1: LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton Société Européenne Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton Société Européenne, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections out to 2035. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €13.1b. Analyst inputs cover the nearer years, and Simply Wall St extrapolates further out, with projected free cash flow of €12.8b in 2029. All figures are in € and kept in billions to reflect the scale of the business.

Pulling these projections together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of €307.09 per share. Against the recent share price of €533.90, this implies the stock is about 73.9% overvalued on this set of cash flow assumptions.

Approach 2: LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton Société Européenne Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay per share directly to the earnings the business is already generating. It lets you quickly see how many euros of price you are paying for each euro of current earnings.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends on how the market views the company’s growth prospects and risk. In general, higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while lower growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower P/E.

LVMH currently trades on a P/E of 24.36x. That is above the Luxury industry average of about 17.14x, but below the peer group average of 52.18x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary Fair Ratio of 28.55x, which is the P/E that would typically be expected given factors such as earnings growth, margins, industry, market value and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the industry because it adjusts for those company characteristics rather than treating all luxury names as identical. Compared with this Fair Ratio, LVMH’s current 24.36x P/E is lower, which points to the shares trading below that modelled level.

Using a line of best fit, we calculated an average annual growth rate of 113%, which signals high confidence from analysts. If this rate turns out to be too aggressive, the company may become profitable much later than analysts predict.

Given this is a high-level overview, we won’t go into details of Fluence's upcoming projects, however, keep in mind that generally a high forecast growth rate is not unusual for a company that is currently undergoing an investment period.

Check out our latest analysis for Fluence

One thing we would like to bring into light with Fluence is it currently has negative equity on its balance sheet. Accounting methods used to deal with losses accumulated over time can cause this to occur. This is because liabilities are carried forward into the future until it cancels. These losses tend to occur only on paper, however, in other cases it can be forewarning.

data.title: Analysts Have Lowered Expectations For PeptiDream Inc. (TSE:4587) After Its Latest Results
data.description:
There's been a notable change in appetite for PeptiDream Inc. (TSE:4587) shares in the week since its yearly report, with the stock down 11% to JP¥1,388. The results look positive overall; while revenues of JP¥19b were in line with analyst predictions, statutory losses were 6.7% smaller than expected, with PeptiDream losing JP¥28.99 per share. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. So we gathered the latest post-earnings forecasts to see what estimates suggest is in store for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

After the latest results, the seven analysts covering PeptiDream are now predicting revenues of JP¥47.9b in 2026. If met, this would reflect a huge 159% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. PeptiDream is also expected to turn profitable, with statutory earnings of JP¥127 per share. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of JP¥56.1b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥156 in 2026. Indeed, we can see that the analysts are a lot more bearish about PeptiDream's prospects following the latest results, administering a real cut to revenue estimates and slashing their EPS estimates to boot.

Check out our latest analysis for PeptiDream

The analysts made no major changes to their price target of JP¥3,008, suggesting the downgrades are not expected to have a long-term impact on PeptiDream's valuation. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. There are some variant perceptions on PeptiDream, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at JP¥4,400 and the most bearish at JP¥2,700 per share. This shows there is still a bit of diversity in estimates, but analysts don't appear to be totally split on the stock as though it might be a success or failure situation.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. It's clear from the latest estimates that PeptiDream's rate of growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully, with the forecast 159% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2026 noticeably faster than its historical growth of 22% p.a. over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 27% per year. It seems obvious that, while the growth outlook is brighter than the recent past, the analysts also expect PeptiDream to grow faster than the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. Regrettably, they also downgraded their revenue estimates, but the latest forecasts still imply the business will grow faster than the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at JP¥3,008, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have estimates - from multiple PeptiDream analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

data.title: Alibaba’s Qwen3.5 AI Puts Open Source And Monetization In Focus
data.description:
• Alibaba Group Holding (NYSE:BABA) has launched its advanced Qwen3.5 AI model, highlighting new multimodal and agentic features.
  • The model is described as cheaper and faster than previous versions, with capabilities that Alibaba says compare with major U.S. AI systems.
  • Alibaba is open sourcing part of its Qwen AI suite and pushing new monetization options for cloud and enterprise customers.

For you as an investor, this move sits at the intersection of Alibaba's core e commerce, cloud, and enterprise software businesses. AI models like Qwen3.5 can plug into logistics, advertising, and customer service, and they can also act as a draw for third party developers using Alibaba Cloud.

The decision to open source parts of Qwen while charging for higher end services reflects a broad ecosystem play rather than a single product bet. How effectively Alibaba converts developer interest into paid usage across its platforms will be an important theme to watch as AI tools become more embedded in global enterprise workflows.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Alibaba Group Holding by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Alibaba Group Holding.

We've flagged 0 risks for Alibaba Group Holding. See which could impact your investment.

For investors, Qwen3.5 looks like Alibaba doubling down on AI as a core product for its e commerce and cloud ecosystems rather than a side project. Management is leaning into cost efficient, high performance models, with Qwen3.5 described as 60% cheaper and capable of running workloads eight times faster than the prior version. That is relevant for cloud customers that are sensitive to total AI compute spend and may see lower deployment costs as a reason to choose Alibaba over peers such as Tencent, Baidu, or U.S. providers.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Alibaba Group Holding Narrative

  • The launch aligns with the existing narrative that heavy AI and cloud investment is intended to support long term revenue growth by making Alibaba a full stack AI service provider across commerce, cloud, and enterprise software.
  • At the same time, sharper AI focus reinforces earlier concerns that high upfront spending on infrastructure and user acquisition, including the Qwen consumer app, can weigh on near term margins and free cash flow.
  • The move to open source a strong version of Qwen3.5 and drive global adoption is only partially reflected in the narrative and could introduce new upside or execution risk depending on how well Alibaba converts open use into paid workloads.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Alibaba Group Holding to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Large AI and cloud investments, including Qwen3.5, continue to put pressure on profitability and may extend the period of weaker margins if monetization is slower than expected.
  • ⚠️ Heightened regulatory and geopolitical scrutiny, including Alibaba being briefly linked to a U.S. military related list, adds reputational and policy risk around sensitive AI and data services.
  • 🎁 Qwen3.5 gives Alibaba a cost focused AI product that can support cloud revenue, defend market share against local rivals like ByteDance and DeepSeek, and deepen integration across its e commerce platforms.
  • 🎁 Analysts already see Qwen as a potential all in one AI assistant, and Qwen models have gained wide open source traction, which may support broader adoption of Alibaba Cloud over time.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth tracking whether Qwen3.5 translates into higher paid cloud workloads, more merchants and brands using AI powered tools inside Alibaba’s platforms, and clearer disclosure on AI related revenue. You can also watch how marketing spend on the Qwen consumer app affects losses in newer segments and whether management maintains its current pace of capital expenditure on AI and quick commerce. Any future updates around international restrictions or regulatory treatment of Chinese AI providers will also be important for judging Alibaba’s room to grow Qwen outside China.
